title: 4.6 AVL Trees
subtitle: AVL balancing principles and rotations (original AVL part + expanded content)

content.introduction: AVL trees enforce strict height balance to guarantee logarithmic-time dynamic set operations.

sections.title: 4.6.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define balance factor and AVL constraint (|balance| <= 1). Explain why local rotations restore global balance.
examples: Compute balance factors for each node
Identify imbalance type from insertion path
Explain single vs double rotation selection

sections.title: 4.6.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe LL, RR, LR, and RL rotations triggered by insertion/deletion imbalance cases.
examples: Perform LL rotation after descending inserts
Perform LR rotation and verify inorder order preserved

sections.title: 4.6.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: AVL guarantees O(log n) search/insert/delete with extra rotation maintenance; compare with less strict balancing schemes.
examples: Compare AVL lookup speed to Red-Black under read-heavy load
Estimate rotation overhead for update-heavy workloads
Choose AVL when search latency is critical

keyTakeaways: Maintain heights and factors after updates
Use AVL for predictable logarithmic operations
